story: In many ancient civilizations, such as those of Egypt and Mesopotamia, architecture and urbanism reflected the constant engagement with the divine and the supernatural, and many ancient cultures resorted to monumentality in architecture to represent symbolically the political power of the ruler, the ruling elite, or the state itself.
q1: In ancient Egypt and Mesopotamia, monumental architecture was used primarily to honor the gods rather than to display political power. (True/False?)
